title: Define routing contract and failing cases
acceptance_criteria: Tests describe candidate cardinality endpoint fallback key selection and terminal audit states
notes: Start with behavior tests before implementation

title: Introduce provider route abstraction
acceptance_criteria: Selection returns route structures instead of flat endpoint key product
notes: Keep business logic injectable and avoid concrete resolver hardcoding

title: Resolve endpoint exact then conversion fallback
acceptance_criteria: Exact endpoints are attempted before conversion and conversion paths are not primary peers

title: Resolve key per attempt with key failover
acceptance_criteria: Multiple keys do not multiply initial candidates and actual selected key is audited
notes: Use stable affinity then deterministic fallback order

title: Finalize audit terminal states
acceptance_criteria: Success and terminal failure leave no dangling available rows
validation_command: cargo check -p backend && cargo test -p storage request_candidate_storage -- --nocapture
notes: Extend current unused handling to failure and cancellation paths

title: Build hierarchical trace UI
acceptance_criteria: Trace shows provider endpoint key retry hierarchy with unused summaries
notes: Keep backend-owned i18n keys in seed files if new labels are needed

title: Make candidate budget explicit
acceptance_criteria: Any topK style limit is explicit configurable and auditable or rejected with recorded rationale
notes: No silent caps; route count remains all eligible provider routes

title: Run end-to-end validation
acceptance_criteria: Full checks pass and a local DB sample proves dot cardinality is bounded
notes: DB sample 019e1f1c-97e4-73e2-b493-4971b0a196f2 wrote one provider-level candidate row instead of old 9-row endpoint retry product
